langchain agents can now pay for remote agent services natively.

pip install langchain-ampersend gives your agent two tools: check what a remote agent can do, then call it. 
when the remote agent returns an http 402, the toolkit handles the full payment flow automatically, authorizes it, signs it with your smart account, and retries the request. your agent never sees the payment logic.

the controls live in AmpersendTreasurer. spend limits, session policies, and analytics are configured there before your agent ever makes a call. so you're not just enabling payments, you're defining what your agent is allowed to spend before it starts.

if you're already building on langchain, this is a one-package addition. your existing agent architecture doesn't change.

read the @LangChain docs ⬇️

hi. i'm a bankr club member.

regarding the income generated from users and agents:

on-chain data shows that bankr is currently generating roughly $69m in annualized revenue. however, the source of this income is heavily skewed:

• token launches: ~99% of revenue comes from token creation and the associated trading fees from those launches. on base, bankr takes a 38% cut of all trading fees (36.1% to bankr + 1.9% to the ecosystem) from every token launched through the platform.
• agent/user swaps: organic trading activity (swaps not related to new launches) generates significantly less, averaging under $200/day.
• daily stats (as of april 18, 2026):
 - launch fees: $11,228.80 (5,805 transactions)
 - swap fees: $62.40 (76 transactions)
 - grand total: $11,291.20 for the day

essentially, while the "conversational agent" and "ai infra" narratives are the front-end, the protocol functions primarily as a high-volume token launchpad. every token deployed through bankr creates a permanent, passive revenue stream for the protocol.

$bnkr to a billion. let's build.

OpenGradient's Titan launch on Virtuals Protocol has opened with strong conviction

To mark the launch, we are allocating an $OPG airdrop to reward Virtuals ecosystem participation. Distribution is weighted across two cohorts:

(1) veVIRTUAL holders based on their net $OPG buys
(2) net $OPG buyers in the post-TGE window

Snapshot and calculation will take place two weeks post-TGE, with distribution claimable directly on each participant's Virtuals profile.

The verifiable AI thesis is landing. 4,000+ models hosted, 2M+ onchain inferences, already live in production through BitQuant, MemSync, and Digital Twins. Exactly the kind of infrastructure the agent economy needed underneath it, and the market is voting with conviction.

What they do:

������ Settle transactions
������ Handle final transfer of value
������ Ensure funds go from A → B

4/ Why this matters?

Most of Web2 focused on rails.

But in an agent-driven world:

������ Payments need to be automated
������ Decisions happen in real-time
������ Interactions are machine-to-machine

And precisely THAT requires protocols.

3/ The key difference

Rails move money.

Protocols decide 'when, why, and how' money moves.
